candidates.why: Revenue component but not total; use as secondary priority in case total revenue label structure changes or is missing in some years.
Explicit top-line revenue total; best representation of total revenues/sales.
This is a cost line, not revenue; should not be used for REVT.
Operating expense, not revenue; excluded for REVT.
Finance income, not operating revenue; excluded for REVT.
Interest income, not core operating revenue; excluded for REVT.

candidates.row_label: Finance income (cost) from remeasurement of balances
candidates.why: Financial remeasurement, not operating revenue; excluded for REVT.

candidates.row_label: Income Available to Com Excl ExtraOrd
candidates.why: Represents net income, not revenue; excluded for REVT.
Comprehensive income, not revenue; excluded for REVT.
Balance sheet liability; not a P&L revenue line for this mapping.

variables.notes: Total revenues is the primary top-line revenue measure. Signing fees and milestone revenues is included as a secondary priority revenue line in case total revenues is missing in some years.

candidates.why: Explicit direct cost of goods sold; best match for COGS.
Alternate label for cost of revenues/COGS with identical values; used as secondary priority.
Likely overhead/indirect costs, not strictly core COGS for this mapping; left out to avoid mixing overhead with direct COGS.
R&D expense, not COGS.
Selling and marketing overhead, not COGS.
General operating expenses, not direct COGS.
Selling/marketing, not COGS.
Overhead travel costs, not COGS.
IP-related overhead, not direct COGS.
Financing costs, not COGS.
Financing costs, not COGS.
Financing item, not COGS.
Broad staff overhead, more SG&A-like than COGS in this context.
Overhead, not clearly direct COGS.
Inventory component on balance sheet, not the P&L COGS line to map.
Liability, not COGS.
Liability, not COGS.

variables.notes: Cost of goods sold is the primary direct cost line; Cost of revenues/COGS is listed as a secondary label with identical values in case label usage changes over time.

candidates.why: Clearly an operating overhead bucket; fits SG&A definition (excluding COGS and D&A).
Personnel-related operating expenses; typically part of SG&A.
Share-based compensation, which should be included in SG&A components.

variables.notes: REVIEWED. SG&A is not presented as a single explicit line; instead, it is broken into multiple operating expense components. Marketing and sales expenses, Other Operating Expenses, Personnel Costs, Share-Based Payment, and STAFF EXPENSES all represent operating overhead (selling, general, admin, and staff-related costs) and exclude COGS and financial items. These selections go beyond the provided candidate list (to add Marketing and sales expenses and STAFF EXPENSES), hence manual review is flagged to confirm that these are not double-counting or overlapping with other classifications in earlier years. | Escape-hatch used (final_choice outside candidate list): [Income Statement] Marketing and sales expenses, [Income Statement] STAFF EXPENSES

candidates.why: Explicit P&L research and development expense line; primary choice for XRD.

candidates.row_label: Amortization of Capitalized Research & Development Expenses - Supplemental
candidates.why: Amortization of previously capitalized R&D; related but not the core expensed R&D line. Kept separate from main XRD.
Alternative R&D expense label, likely used in some years; good secondary line for XRD.
Balance sheet asset, not a P&L R&D expense; not used for XRD.

candidates.row_label: Product Development Costs - Accumulated Amortization & Impairment
candidates.why: Balance sheet contra-asset; not the P&L expense line to map for XRD.
Balance sheet asset; not XRD expense.

variables.notes: Research and development expenses is the main R&D expense line; Research & Development Exp is included as an alternative label that may be used in other periods. Balance sheet product development items are not used for XRD.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities
candidates.why: Interest component of lease liabilities; a type of interest expense and valid for XINT if more generic labels are missing.
Generic interest expense line; primary choice for XINT.
Interest-like cost related to earnout liability; relevant interest expense component.
Represents minority interests, not interest expense; should not be used for XINT.
Represents minority interests, not interest expense; should not be used for XINT.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental
candidates.why: Supplemental disclosure of lease interest; valid fallback interest expense line.

final_choice.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities
Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental

variables.notes: Interest Expense is the primary generic interest expense line. Interest costs earnout and the lease-related interest expense lines are included as secondary priorities to capture interest expense across all years and instrument types. Minority interest lines are correctly excluded.

candidates.why: Label suggests total shareholders’ equity, but appears unused/empty in the preview; a possible alternate equity label.
Numeric line representing shareholders’ equity; best match for book equity attributable to owners, with Minority Int. shown separately and zero.
Combined equity and minority interest; would double-count MIB if used for BE when a separate shareholders equity line exists.
Balancing item, not a primary reported equity figure.

candidates.row_label: Balancing Item - Total Liability and Equity
candidates.why: Balancing row, not equity itself.
Total of equity plus liabilities; not pure equity.

variables.notes: Shareholders equity is selected as book equity attributable to owners; Minority Int. is reported separately and appears to be zero, so using the shareholders equity line avoids double-counting any non-controlling interests.

candidates.why: Income statement minority interests; however, MIB should be mapped to the balance sheet minority interest for equity calculations.
Income statement minority interest; not used directly for the equity MIB mapping.
Balance sheet minority interest/non-controlling interests line; best match for MIB even if currently zero in preview.
Combined total equity including minority interest; using this would mix BE and MIB and risk double counting.

variables.notes: Minority Int. on the balance sheet is the appropriate line for non-controlling interests (MIB). Even if currently zero in the preview, it is mapped to allow for nonzero values in other periods.
